Another 300U of DpnII was added into the system and samples were further incubated at 37℃ for 2-4h with shaking at 950rpm. Samples were then incubated at 65℃ for 20min with shaking at 950rpm to inactivate DpnII. Nuclei was pelleted, cooled down to room temperature, re-suspended in 300ul 1xNEB buffer2, containing 50uM biotin-14-dATP (Thermal Fisher Scientific, 19524016), dGTP, dCTP, dTTP (Promega, U121B, U122B, U123B) and 40U DNA Polymerase I, Large (Klenow) fragment (NEB, M0210) and incubated at 37℃ for 1.5h with shaking at 950rpm. The system was then added with 120ul 10xT4 DNA ligase buffer, 12ul 100xBSA, 758ul nuclease free water and 10ul (4000U) T4 DNA ligase (NEB, M0202M). Ligation was carried out at 16℃ for 4h and room temperature for additional 2h. 20ul of 20mg/ml protease K (3115879 BMB) and 120ul of 10%SDS were added into the system and samples were de-crosslinked at 65℃ for overnight. An additional 10ul of protease K was added and samples were further de-crosslinked at 55℃ for 2h. DNase-free RNase was added into the system and samples were incubated at 37℃ for 30min. Ligated DNA was extracted with phenol chloroform extraction and re-constituted in 180ul nuclease free water. DNA concentration was quantified with a Qubit 4 Fluorometer (Thermal Fisher Scientific, Q33226). Ligated DNA was sonicated to 200-300bp (Epishear, Active Motif, 100% amplitude, 30s ON and 30s OFF, 25-30min) and cleaned up with AMPure XP beads (Beckman Coulter). Each sample of biotinylated DNA was pulled down with 100ul Dynabeads MyOne Streptavidin C1 beads (Thermal Fisher Scientific, 65002) by incubating at room temperature for 15min. The amount of bound DNA was calculated by subtracting the amount of unbound DNA from the amount of total DNA. DNA bound streptavidin beads were washed once with 1xbead wash buffer (5mM Tris-HCl (pH 7.5), 0.5mM EDTA and 1M NaCl) and once with T4 DNA ligase buffer. Streptavidin beads were re-suspended with 84ml water. Library was prepared using NEBNext DNA Library Prep Master Mix Set for Illumina (NEB E6040, M0543L, E7335S) according to manufacture instruction. On-bead end repair was performed by mixing 84ul biotinylated beads with 10ul NEBNext End Repair Reaction Buffer (10x), 5ul NEBNext End Repair Enzyme Mix and 5U DNA Polymerase I, Large (Klenow) Fragment (NEB, M0210). The mixture was incubated at room temperature for 30min. Beads with end repaired blunt DNA was washed once with 600ul 1xbead wash buffer and then 500ul 1xNEB buffer2. Beads was re-suspended in 42ul nuclease free water. Beads were then mixed with 5ul NEBNext dA-tailing reaction buffe (10x) and 3ul Klenow Fragment (3’→5’ exo-) for dA-tailing. The mixture was incubated at 37℃ for 30min. Beads were then washed once with 600ul 1xbead wash buffer and then 600ul 1xT4 DNA ligase buffer. Beads were re-suspended in 20ul nuclease free water. Adaptor ligation was performed by mixing the beads with 10ul Quick Ligation reaction buffer (5x), 15ul NEBNext Adaptor (15uM) and 5ul Quick T4 ligase. The mixture was incubated at room temperature for 15min. 3ul of USER enzyme was then added into the mixture and incubated at 37℃ for 15min. Beads were washed twice with 600ul 1xbead wash buffer and re-suspended in 50ul nuclease free water containing 0.1% SDS. Beads were incubated at 98℃ for 10min to release DNA and DNA was cleaned up with AMPure XP beads. Purified adaptor ligated DNA was then index labeled with NEBNext multiplex oligos for 6 cycles on a thermal cycler. PCR products were purified using AMPure XP beads. Sequencing was performed on an Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using Illumina sequencing reagents according to manufacturer’s instructions.

Sample.Data-Processing: Paired-end reads were aligned independently to mm9 mouse genome using bowtie2 (global parameters: --very-sensitive –L 30 –score-min L,-0.6,-0.2 –end-to-end --reorder; local parameters: --very-sensitive –L 20 –score-min L,-0.6,-0.2 –end-to-end --reorder) through the HiC-Pro software. Unmapped reads, non-uniquely mapped reads, and PCR duplicates were filtered, and uniquely aligned reads were paired. Reads from across sequencing runs and replicates (Rep1, Rep2) were pooled, then used to construct raw contact matrices at 10 kb resolution. HiC preprocessing data processing step: Paired-end reads were aligned independently to mm9 mouse genome using bowtie2 (global parameters: --very-sensitive –L 30 –score-min L,-0.6,-0.2 –end-to-end --reorder; local parameters: --very-sensitive –L 20 –score-min L,-0.6,-0.2 –end-to-end --reorder) through the HiC-Pro software. Unmapped reads, non-uniquely mapped reads, and PCR duplicates were filtered, and uniquely aligned reads were paired. Reads from across sequencing runs and replicates (Rep1, Rep2) were pooled, then used to construct raw contact matrices at 10 kb resolution. HiC domain calling data processing step: Raw contact matrices were balanced using the Knight-Ruiz matrix balancing algorithm and quantile normalized across all five timepoints (prometa, anatelo, earlyG1, midG1, and lateG1). Domains were then called using 3DNetMod with published parameters. Consistent parameter adjustments were implemented across all time points: 0 minutes, 25 minutes, 60 minutes, 120 minutes, and 240 minutes on quantile normalized merged data at 10 kb resolution. Genome-wide counts data were logged and chunked into 7.5 MB regions with 5 MB overlap. A permissive filter threshold maximum 500 kb consecutive zeros in diagonal and minimum 2/3 nonzero counts coverage in central diagonal of chunked region was used for region acceptance (all regions except empty regions at beginning and end of chromosome or centromere/telomeres). For gamma selection, a restrictive minimum “plateau” size of 30 was used to induce more stable persistent calls (i.e. at least 30 consecutive gamma values, every 0.01 gamma step, with same mean number of called domains per 20 attempts, at each successive gamma). Optimal mean gamma value of each plateau was then selected for hierarchical domain detection. An adjusted rand index (arand) consensus call was then determined from a partition size of 20 attempts for each selected plateau gamma value. Additionally, a minimum domain size threshold of 150 kb was selected. For post processing, a restrictive maximum partition block variance threshold for called domains of 12 bins around each domain boundary across all sizes was chosen. Final initial domain calls per time point underwent a merging step, wherein calls within a single bin at both boundaries were subsumed into a single call with a merged start boundary declared at furthest upstream and stop boundary declared at furthest downstream of merged calls. HiC stripe calling data processing step: Raw contact matrices were filtered to remove regions of low mappability and extreme outliers, then balanced using the Knight-Ruiz matrix balancing algorithm. We then applied a modified version of the stripe calling approach from (Vian et al. 2018). Briefly, we computed local horizontal and vertical signals from the raw contact matrix by finding the median of 10 pixels orthogonal to each query pixel. For the vertical signals, the median was calculated with respect to 5 pixels directly above and below, and for the horizontal signals, the median was calculated with respect to 5 pixels directly left and right. We then evaluated the local neighborhood around each pixel by convolving over the balanced contact matrix using top, bottom, left, and right footprints. Then, we computed the vertical and horizontal signals in the balanced matrix using the same logic as used for the raw matrix to generate bias factors that allow deconvolution of the directional footprints for statistical testing. Finally, to compute the expected background signal, we multiplied the left and right values by the bias factor and selected the maximum value. To compute the expected background signal, we multiplied the top and bottom values by their own bias factor instead. We assumed that the horizontal and vertical signals followed independent Poisson distributions parameterized by their background expected signals, testing whether the observed horizontal and vertical signals were higher than their expected signals. After obtaining Poisson p-values, we filtered and clustered significant pixels into stripes. First, we filtered out pixels with p-values greater than 0.15 and fold-changes less than 1.5. We also discarded pixels that had observed values less than 2 in the balanced contact matrix. After filtering, we clustered directly adjacent pixels in the vertical and horizontal directions separately. Horizontal and vertical clusters containing less than 3 pixels were thrown out. Then, we carried out a second clustering step where we merged surviving clusters with nearby clusters in the same direction. The maximum allowed distance between two clusters was 20 pixels. Once nearby clusters were merged, only those with 12 pixels or more were kept. Then, directly adjacent clusters in the orthogonal direction were merged together. The maximum cluster width was capped at 5 pixels. HiC loop calling data processing step: Raw contact matrices were filtered to remove regions of low mappability and extreme outliers, then balanced using the Knight-Ruiz matrix balancing algorithm. Expected models were constructed using the maximum of the donut and lower left filters for pixels with interaction distances between 100 kb and 10 Mb, and our new “upper triangular” donut filter for pixels with interaction distances within 100 kb. P-values were computed for each pixel under a Poisson model, comparing the raw count for each pixel with the deconvoluted (using the Knight-Ruiz bias vectors) expected value for that pixel. Q-values were computed for each pixel using a combination of lambda-chunking and BH-FDR. Clusters of at least three neighboring significant pixels were identified and refined by applying progressively more stringent q-value thresholds, starting from the set of pixels significant at a FDR of 1%. Pixels with low observed counts or fold change between observed and expected values were removed from consideration prior to clustering. Refined clusters including pixels with interaction distances of 20 kb or less were removed. HiC processed data files format and content: The processed data files <timepoint>_<stage>_intrachromosomal_contact_matrices.tar.gz are gzipped tarballs containing the 10kb resolution contact matrices for the pooled HiC libraries for <timepoint> (prometa, anatelo, earlyG1, midG1, or lateG1) at the <stage> (raw, kr, or qnorm) step of the data processing. The tarballs contain files <timepoint>_<stage>_<chromosome>.matrix, which contain the intrachromosomal contact matrix for each chromosome. Each line of a .matrix file represents an interaction between two bins on that chromosome. The .matrix files are formatted as tab delimited files with three columns, which represent the start coordinate of the upstream bin, the start coordinate of the downstream bin, and the value of the interaction, respectively. The end coordinates of the bins can be determined by adding the matrix resolution (10000 or 40000) to the start coordinate. For example, the line "10000<tab>40000<tab>10" in a 10kb resolution matrix file for chr1 should be interpreted to mean that the bin chr1:10000-20000 interacts with the bin chr1:40000-50000 with quantitative value 10. Bin-bin pairs with zero reads, or values that have been filtered out during processing are not listed in the matrix files. Therefore not every possible bin-bin pair has a corresponding line in the matrix file. Such bin-bin pairs can be assumed to

Sample.Title: ChIP-seq-CTCF-asyn-rep1

Channel.Treatment-Protocol: Cells were synchronized to pro-metaphase with 200ng/ml nocodazole for 7-8.5h. For post-mitotic enrichment, cells were released from nocodazole for 0, 25min, 60min, 120min and 240min for pro-meta, ana/telo, early-G1, mid-G1 and late-G1 respectively.
Channel.Growth-Protocol: G1E-ER4 is a subline of the GATA1-null proerythroblast cell line G1E (Weiss et al, Mol. Cell. Biol. 17, 1642–51 (1997).). Cells were cultered in IMDM medium, supplemented with 15% FBS, penicillin/streptomycin, kit ligand, monothioglycerol and Epoetin alpha at 37 degrees with 5% CO2 (as described in Weiss et al., 1997).
Channel.Extract-Protocol: For each IP, 7-10million crosslinked cells were re-suspended in 1ml Cell Lysis Buffer (10mM Tris pH 8, 10mM NaCl, 0.2% NP-40/Igepal) containing fresh protease inhibitors (Sigma P8340) and 1mM phenylmethylsulfonyl fluoride (PMSF) for 20min on ice. Nuclei was pelleted at 4℃ and re-suspended in 1ml Nuclear Lysis Buffer (50mM Tris pH 8, 10mM EDTA, 1% SDS) containing fresh protease inhibitors and PMSF and incubated for 20min on ice. Samples were added with 0.6ml IP Dilution Buffer (20mM Tris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 150mM NaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.01% SDS) containing fresh protease inhibitor and PMSF and then subject to sonication for 45min (Epishear, Active Motif, 100% amplitude, 30s ON and 30s OFF, ~45min). After sonication, samples were spun at 21130g at 4℃ for 10min to pellet cell debris. Supernatant was transferred to a new tube and supplemented with 3.4ml IP Dilution Buffer containing fresh protease inhibitor, PMSF, protein A/G agarose beads (prepared by mixing protein A (Thermo Fisher Scientific 15918014) and protein G (Thermo Fisher Scientific 15920010) agarose beads at 1:1 ratio) and 50mg isotope matched IgG. Chromatin preclear was carried out for over 2h at 4℃. 200ml (4%) of precleared chromatin was taken out from subsequent IP steps and set as input. Pre-cleared chromatin was then added to protein A/C beads bound with antibodies and incubated overnight with rotation at 4℃. After IP, beads were washed once with IP Wash Buffer I (20mM Tris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 50mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.1% SDS), twice with High Salt Buffer (20mMTris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 500mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.01% SDS), once with IP Wash Buffer II (10mMTris pH 8, 1mM EDTA, 0.25 M LiCl, 1% NP-40/Igepal, 1% sodium deoxycholate) and twice with PE Buffer (10mM Tris pH 8, 1mM EDTA pH 8). All washing and pelleting steps were performed on ice or at 4℃. Beads were then moved to room temperature and eluted twice with 100ml freshly prepared Elution Buffer (100mM NaHCO3, 1%SDS) to reach a final concentration of 200ml. Eluted samples were supplemented with 12ml 5M NaCl, 2ml DNase-free RNaseA (10mg/ml, 10109169001 BMB) and incubated at 65℃ for overnight. Samples were then added with 3ml protease K (20mg/ml, 3115879 BMB) and further de-crosslinked at 65℃ for 2h. De-crosslinked DNA was added with 10ml 3M sodium acetate pH 5.2 and purified with QIAquick PCR Purification kit (QIAGEN 28106) according to manufacture instruction. Library construction was performed using Illumina’s TruSeq ChIP sample preparation kit (Illumina, catalog no. IP-202-1012) according to manufacturer’s specifications with the addition of size selection (left side at 0.9x, right side at 0.6x) using SPRIselect beads (Beckman Coulter, catalog no. B23318) prior to PCR amplification. Library size was determined (average 351 bp, range 333-372 bp) using the Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100, followed by quantitation using real-time PCR using the KAPA Library Quant Kit for Illumina (KAPA Biosystems catalog no. KK4835). Libraries were then pooled and sequenced on the Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using Illumina sequencing reagents according to manufacturer’s instructions

Sample.Data-Processing: Sequencing reads were mapped to the reference mouse genome mm9 using bowtie (0.12.7, "-m 2 --tryhard"). Reads were filtered to remove non-uniquely mapped reads and PCR duplicates using SAMtools (v1.7, "samtools rmdup -s") and converted to bed format using BEDtools (v2.27.1, "bedtools bamtobed"). Peaks were identified using the MACS2 (v2.1.0, "macs2 callpeak") with punctate calling for both CTCF and Rad21 (p-values 1e-8 and 1e-4 respectively), using each IP's cell cycle stage matched input as the control. Genome_build: mm9

Channel.Growth-Protocol: G1E-ER4 is a subline of the GATA1-null proerythroblast cell line G1E (Weiss et al, Mol. Cell. Biol. 17, 1642–51 (1997).). Cells were cultered in IMDM medium, supplemented with 15% FBS, penicillin/streptomycin, kit ligand, monothioglycerol and Epoetin alpha at 37 degrees with 5% CO2 (as described in Weiss et al., 1997).
Channel.Extract-Protocol: For each IP, 7-10million crosslinked cells were re-suspended in 1ml Cell Lysis Buffer (10mM Tris pH 8, 10mM NaCl, 0.2% NP-40/Igepal) containing fresh protease inhibitors (Sigma P8340) and 1mM phenylmethylsulfonyl fluoride (PMSF) for 20min on ice. Nuclei was pelleted at 4℃ and re-suspended in 1ml Nuclear Lysis Buffer (50mM Tris pH 8, 10mM EDTA, 1% SDS) containing fresh protease inhibitors and PMSF and incubated for 20min on ice. Samples were added with 0.6ml IP Dilution Buffer (20mM Tris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 150mM NaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.01% SDS) containing fresh protease inhibitor and PMSF and then subject to sonication for 45min (Epishear, Active Motif, 100% amplitude, 30s ON and 30s OFF, ~45min). After sonication, samples were spun at 21130g at 4℃ for 10min to pellet cell debris. Supernatant was transferred to a new tube and supplemented with 3.4ml IP Dilution Buffer containing fresh protease inhibitor, PMSF, protein A/G agarose beads (prepared by mixing protein A (Thermo Fisher Scientific 15918014) and protein G (Thermo Fisher Scientific 15920010) agarose beads at 1:1 ratio) and 50mg isotope matched IgG. Chromatin preclear was carried out for over 2h at 4℃. 200ml (4%) of precleared chromatin was taken out from subsequent IP steps and set as input. Pre-cleared chromatin was then added to protein A/C beads bound with antibodies and incubated overnight with rotation at 4℃. After IP, beads were washed once with IP Wash Buffer I (20mM Tris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 50mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.1% SDS), twice with High Salt Buffer (20mMTris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 500mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.01% SDS), once with IP Wash Buffer II (10mMTris pH 8, 1mM EDTA, 0.25 M LiCl, 1% NP-40/Igepal, 1% sodium deoxycholate) and twice with PE Buffer (10mM Tris pH 8, 1mM EDTA pH 8). All washing and pelleting steps were performed on ice or at 4℃. Beads were then moved to room temperature and eluted twice with 100ml freshly prepared Elution Buffer (100mM NaHCO3, 1%SDS) to reach a final concentration of 200ml. Eluted samples were supplemented with 12ml 5M NaCl, 2ml DNase-free RNaseA (10mg/ml, 10109169001 BMB) and incubated at 65℃ for overnight. Samples were then added with 3ml protease K (20mg/ml, 3115879 BMB) and further de-crosslinked at 65℃ for 2h. De-crosslinked DNA was added with 10ml 3M sodium acetate pH 5.2 and purified with QIAquick PCR Purification kit (QIAGEN 28106) according to manufacture instruction. Library construction was performed using Illumina’s TruSeq ChIP sample preparation kit (Illumina, catalog no. IP-202-1012) according to manufacturer’s specifications with the addition of size selection (left side at 0.9x, right side at 0.6x) using SPRIselect beads (Beckman Coulter, catalog no. B23318) prior to PCR amplification. Library size was determined (average 351 bp, range 333-372 bp) using the Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100, followed by quantitation using real-time PCR using the KAPA Library Quant Kit for Illumina (KAPA Biosystems catalog no. KK4835). Libraries were then pooled and sequenced on the Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using Illumina sequencing reagents according to manufacturer’s instructions

Sample.Data-Processing: Sequencing reads were mapped to the reference mouse genome mm9 using bowtie (0.12.7, "-m 2 --tryhard"). Reads were filtered to remove non-uniquely mapped reads and PCR duplicates using SAMtools (v1.7, "samtools rmdup -s") and converted to bed format using BEDtools (v2.27.1, "bedtools bamtobed"). Peaks were identified using the MACS2 (v2.1.0, "macs2 callpeak") with punctate calling for both CTCF and Rad21 (p-values 1e-8 and 1e-4 respectively), using each IP's cell cycle stage matched input as the control. Genome_build: mm9

Pre-cleared chromatin was then added to protein A/C beads bound with antibodies and incubated overnight with rotation at 4℃. After IP, beads were washed once with IP Wash Buffer I (20mM Tris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 50mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.1% SDS), twice with High Salt Buffer (20mMTris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 500mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.01% SDS), once with IP Wash Buffer II (10mMTris pH 8, 1mM EDTA, 0.25 M LiCl, 1% NP-40/Igepal, 1% sodium deoxycholate) and twice with PE Buffer (10mM Tris pH 8, 1mM EDTA pH 8). All washing and pelleting steps were performed on ice or at 4℃. Beads were then moved to room temperature and eluted twice with 100ml freshly prepared Elution Buffer (100mM NaHCO3, 1%SDS) to reach a final concentration of 200ml. Eluted samples were supplemented with 12ml 5M NaCl, 2ml DNase-free RNaseA (10mg/ml, 10109169001 BMB) and incubated at 65℃ for overnight. Samples were then added with 3ml protease K (20mg/ml, 3115879 BMB) and further de-crosslinked at 65℃ for 2h. De-crosslinked DNA was added with 10ml 3M sodium acetate pH 5.2 and purified with QIAquick PCR Purification kit (QIAGEN 28106) according to manufacture instruction. Library construction was performed using Illumina’s TruSeq ChIP sample preparation kit (Illumina, catalog no. IP-202-1012) according to manufacturer’s specifications with the addition of size selection (left side at 0.9x, right side at 0.6x) using SPRIselect beads (Beckman Coulter, catalog no. B23318) prior to PCR amplification. Library size was determined (average 351 bp, range 333-372 bp) using the Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100, followed by quantitation using real-time PCR using the KAPA Library Quant Kit for Illumina (KAPA Biosystems catalog no. KK4835). Libraries were then pooled and sequenced on the Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using Illumina sequencing reagents according to manufacturer’s instructions

Sample.Data-Processing: Sequencing reads were mapped to the reference mouse genome mm9 using bowtie (0.12.7, "-m 2 --tryhard"). Reads were filtered to remove non-uniquely mapped reads and PCR duplicates using SAMtools (v1.7, "samtools rmdup -s") and converted to bed format using BEDtools (v2.27.1, "bedtools bamtobed"). Peaks were identified using the MACS2 (v2.1.0, "macs2 callpeak") with punctate calling for both CTCF and Rad21 (p-values 1e-8 and 1e-4 respectively), using each IP's cell cycle stage matched input as the control. Genome_build: mm9

IP-202-1012) according to manufacturer’s specifications with the addition of size selection (left side at 0.9x, right side at 0.6x) using SPRIselect beads (Beckman Coulter, catalog no. B23318) prior to PCR amplification. Library size was determined (average 351 bp, range 333-372 bp) using the Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100, followed by quantitation using real-time PCR using the KAPA Library Quant Kit for Illumina (KAPA Biosystems catalog no. KK4835). Libraries were then pooled and sequenced on the Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using Illumina sequencing reagents according to manufacturer’s instructions

Sample.Data-Processing: Sequencing reads were mapped to the reference mouse genome mm9 using bowtie (0.12.7, "-m 2 --tryhard"). Reads were filtered to remove non-uniquely mapped reads and PCR duplicates using SAMtools (v1.7, "samtools rmdup -s") and converted to bed format using BEDtools (v2.27.1, "bedtools bamtobed"). Peaks were identified using the MACS2 (v2.1.0, "macs2 callpeak") with punctate calling for both CTCF and Rad21 (p-values 1e-8 and 1e-4 respectively), using each IP's cell cycle stage matched input as the control. Genome_build: mm9

Sample.Title: ChIP-seq-Rad21-ana.telo-rep1

Channel.Treatment-Protocol: Cells were synchronized to pro-metaphase with 200ng/ml nocodazole for 7-8.5h. For post-mitotic enrichment, cells were released from nocodazole for 0, 25min, 60min, 120min and 240min for pro-meta, ana/telo, early-G1, mid-G1 and late-G1 respectively.
Channel.Growth-Protocol: G1E-ER4 is a subline of the GATA1-null proerythroblast cell line G1E (Weiss et al, Mol. Cell. Biol. 17, 1642–51 (1997).). Cells were cultered in IMDM medium, supplemented with 15% FBS, penicillin/streptomycin, kit ligand, monothioglycerol and Epoetin alpha at 37 degrees with 5% CO2 (as described in Weiss et al., 1997).
Channel.Extract-Protocol: For each IP, 7-10million crosslinked cells were re-suspended in 1ml Cell Lysis Buffer (10mM Tris pH 8, 10mM NaCl, 0.2% NP-40/Igepal) containing fresh protease inhibitors (Sigma P8340) and 1mM phenylmethylsulfonyl fluoride (PMSF) for 20min on ice. Nuclei was pelleted at 4℃ and re-suspended in 1ml Nuclear Lysis Buffer (50mM Tris pH 8, 10mM EDTA, 1% SDS) containing fresh protease inhibitors and PMSF and incubated for 20min on ice. Samples were added with 0.6ml IP Dilution Buffer (20mM Tris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 150mM NaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.01% SDS) containing fresh protease inhibitor and PMSF and then subject to sonication for 45min (Epishear, Active Motif, 100% amplitude, 30s ON and 30s OFF, ~45min). After sonication, samples were spun at 21130g at 4℃ for 10min to pellet cell debris. Supernatant was transferred to a new tube and supplemented with 3.4ml IP Dilution Buffer containing fresh protease inhibitor, PMSF, protein A/G agarose beads (prepared by mixing protein A (Thermo Fisher Scientific 15918014) and protein G (Thermo Fisher Scientific 15920010) agarose beads at 1:1 ratio) and 50mg isotope matched IgG. Chromatin preclear was carried out for over 2h at 4℃. 200ml (4%) of precleared chromatin was taken out from subsequent IP steps and set as input. Pre-cleared chromatin was then added to protein A/C beads bound with antibodies and incubated overnight with rotation at 4℃. After IP, beads were washed once with IP Wash Buffer I (20mM Tris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 50mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.1% SDS), twice with High Salt Buffer (20mMTris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 500mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.01% SDS), once with IP Wash Buffer II (10mMTris pH 8, 1mM EDTA, 0.25 M LiCl, 1% NP-40/Igepal, 1% sodium deoxycholate) and twice with PE Buffer (10mM Tris pH 8, 1mM EDTA pH 8). All washing and pelleting steps were performed on ice or at 4℃. Beads were then moved to room temperature and eluted twice with 100ml freshly prepared Elution Buffer (100mM NaHCO3, 1%SDS) to reach a final concentration of 200ml. Eluted samples were supplemented with 12ml 5M NaCl, 2ml DNase-free RNaseA (10mg/ml, 10109169001 BMB) and incubated at 65℃ for overnight. Samples were then added with 3ml protease K (20mg/ml, 3115879 BMB) and further de-crosslinked at 65℃ for 2h. De-crosslinked DNA was added with 10ml 3M sodium acetate pH 5.2 and purified with QIAquick PCR Purification kit (QIAGEN 28106) according to manufacture instruction. Library construction was performed using Illumina’s TruSeq ChIP sample preparation kit (Illumina, catalog no. IP-202-1012) according to manufacturer’s specifications with the addition of size selection (left side at 0.9x, right side at 0.6x) using SPRIselect beads (Beckman Coulter, catalog no. B23318) prior to PCR amplification. Library size was determined (average 351 bp, range 333-372 bp) using the Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100, followed by quantitation using real-time PCR using the KAPA Library Quant Kit for Illumina (KAPA Biosystems catalog no. KK4835). Libraries were then pooled and sequenced on the Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using Illumina sequencing reagents according to manufacturer’s instructions

Sample.Title: ChIP-seq-Rad21-late-G1-rep1

Channel.Treatment-Protocol: Cells were synchronized to pro-metaphase with 200ng/ml nocodazole for 7-8.5h. For post-mitotic enrichment, cells were released from nocodazole for 0, 25min, 60min, 120min and 240min for pro-meta, ana/telo, early-G1, mid-G1 and late-G1 respectively.
Channel.Growth-Protocol: G1E-ER4 is a subline of the GATA1-null proerythroblast cell line G1E (Weiss et al, Mol. Cell. Biol. 17, 1642–51 (1997).). Cells were cultered in IMDM medium, supplemented with 15% FBS, penicillin/streptomycin, kit ligand, monothioglycerol and Epoetin alpha at 37 degrees with 5% CO2 (as described in Weiss et al., 1997).
Channel.Extract-Protocol: For each IP, 7-10million crosslinked cells were re-suspended in 1ml Cell Lysis Buffer (10mM Tris pH 8, 10mM NaCl, 0.2% NP-40/Igepal) containing fresh protease inhibitors (Sigma P8340) and 1mM phenylmethylsulfonyl fluoride (PMSF) for 20min on ice. Nuclei was pelleted at 4℃ and re-suspended in 1ml Nuclear Lysis Buffer (50mM Tris pH 8, 10mM EDTA, 1% SDS) containing fresh protease inhibitors and PMSF and incubated for 20min on ice. Samples were added with 0.6ml IP Dilution Buffer (20mM Tris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 150mM NaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.01% SDS) containing fresh protease inhibitor and PMSF and then subject to sonication for 45min (Epishear, Active Motif, 100% amplitude, 30s ON and 30s OFF, ~45min). After sonication, samples were spun at 21130g at 4℃ for 10min to pellet cell debris. Supernatant was transferred to a new tube and supplemented with 3.4ml IP Dilution Buffer containing fresh protease inhibitor, PMSF, protein A/G agarose beads (prepared by mixing protein A (Thermo Fisher Scientific 15918014) and protein G (Thermo Fisher Scientific 15920010) agarose beads at 1:1 ratio) and 50mg isotope matched IgG. Chromatin preclear was carried out for over 2h at 4℃. 200ml (4%) of precleared chromatin was taken out from subsequent IP steps and set as input. Pre-cleared chromatin was then added to protein A/C beads bound with antibodies and incubated overnight with rotation at 4℃. After IP, beads were washed once with IP Wash Buffer I (20mM Tris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 50mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.1% SDS), twice with High Salt Buffer (20mMTris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 500mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.01% SDS), once with IP Wash Buffer II (10mMTris pH 8, 1mM EDTA, 0.25 M LiCl, 1% NP-40/Igepal, 1% sodium deoxycholate) and twice with PE Buffer (10mM Tris pH 8, 1mM EDTA pH 8). All washing and pelleting steps were performed on ice or at 4℃. Beads were then moved to room temperature and eluted twice with 100ml freshly prepared Elution Buffer (100mM NaHCO3, 1%SDS) to reach a final concentration of 200ml. Eluted samples were supplemented with 12ml 5M NaCl, 2ml DNase-free RNaseA (10mg/ml, 10109169001 BMB) and incubated at 65℃ for overnight. Samples were then added with 3ml protease K (20mg/ml, 3115879 BMB) and further de-crosslinked at 65℃ for 2h. De-crosslinked DNA was added with 10ml 3M sodium acetate pH 5.2 and purified with QIAquick PCR Purification kit (QIAGEN 28106) according to manufacture instruction. Library construction was performed using Illumina’s TruSeq ChIP sample preparation kit (Illumina, catalog no. IP-202-1012) according to manufacturer’s specifications with the addition of size selection (left side at 0.9x, right side at 0.6x) using SPRIselect beads (Beckman Coulter, catalog no. B23318) prior to PCR amplification. Library size was determined (average 351 bp, range 333-372 bp) using the Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100, followed by quantitation using real-time PCR using the KAPA Library Quant Kit for Illumina (KAPA Biosystems catalog no. KK4835). Libraries were then pooled and sequenced on the Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using Illumina sequencing reagents according to manufacturer’s instructions

Sample.Data-Processing: Sequencing reads were mapped to the reference mouse genome mm9 using bowtie (0.12.7, "-m 2 --tryhard"). Reads were filtered to remove non-uniquely mapped reads and PCR duplicates using SAMtools (v1.7, "samtools rmdup -s") and converted to bed format using BEDtools (v2.27.1, "bedtools bamtobed"). Peaks were identified using the MACS2 (v2.1.0, "macs2 callpeak") with punctate calling for both CTCF and Rad21 (p-values 1e-8 and 1e-4 respectively), using each IP's cell cycle stage matched input as the control. Genome_build: mm9

Supernatant was transferred to a new tube and supplemented with 3.4ml IP Dilution Buffer containing fresh protease inhibitor, PMSF, protein A/G agarose beads (prepared by mixing protein A (Thermo Fisher Scientific 15918014) and protein G (Thermo Fisher Scientific 15920010) agarose beads at 1:1 ratio) and 50mg isotope matched IgG. Chromatin preclear was carried out for over 2h at 4℃. 200ml (4%) of precleared chromatin was taken out from subsequent IP steps and set as input. Pre-cleared chromatin was then added to protein A/C beads bound with antibodies and incubated overnight with rotation at 4℃. After IP, beads were washed once with IP Wash Buffer I (20mM Tris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 50mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.1% SDS), twice with High Salt Buffer (20mMTris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 500mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.01% SDS), once with IP Wash Buffer II (10mMTris pH 8, 1mM EDTA, 0.25 M LiCl, 1% NP-40/Igepal, 1% sodium deoxycholate) and twice with PE Buffer (10mM Tris pH 8, 1mM EDTA pH 8). All washing and pelleting steps were performed on ice or at 4℃. Beads were then moved to room temperature and eluted twice with 100ml freshly prepared Elution Buffer (100mM NaHCO3, 1%SDS) to reach a final concentration of 200ml. Eluted samples were supplemented with 12ml 5M NaCl, 2ml DNase-free RNaseA (10mg/ml, 10109169001 BMB) and incubated at 65℃ for overnight. Samples were then added with 3ml protease K (20mg/ml, 3115879 BMB) and further de-crosslinked at 65℃ for 2h. De-crosslinked DNA was added with 10ml 3M sodium acetate pH 5.2 and purified with QIAquick PCR Purification kit (QIAGEN 28106) according to manufacture instruction. Library construction was performed using Illumina’s TruSeq ChIP sample preparation kit (Illumina, catalog no. IP-202-1012) according to manufacturer’s specifications with the addition of size selection (left side at 0.9x, right side at 0.6x) using SPRIselect beads (Beckman Coulter, catalog no. B23318) prior to PCR amplification. Library size was determined (average 351 bp, range 333-372 bp) using the Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100, followed by quantitation using real-time PCR using the KAPA Library Quant Kit for Illumina (KAPA Biosystems catalog no. KK4835). Libraries were then pooled and sequenced on the Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using Illumina sequencing reagents according to manufacturer’s instructions

Sample.Data-Processing: Sequencing reads were mapped to the reference mouse genome mm9 using bowtie (0.12.7, "-m 2 --tryhard"). Reads were filtered to remove non-uniquely mapped reads and PCR duplicates using SAMtools (v1.7, "samtools rmdup -s") and converted to bed format using BEDtools (v2.27.1, "bedtools bamtobed"). Peaks were identified using the MACS2 (v2.1.0, "macs2 callpeak") with punctate calling for both CTCF and Rad21 (p-values 1e-8 and 1e-4 respectively), using each IP's cell cycle stage matched input as the control. Genome_build: mm9

Sample.Title: ChIP-seq-input-late-G1

Library construction was performed using Illumina’s TruSeq ChIP sample preparation kit (Illumina, catalog no. IP-202-1012) according to manufacturer’s specifications with the addition of size selection (left side at 0.9x, right side at 0.6x) using SPRIselect beads (Beckman Coulter, catalog no. B23318) prior to PCR amplification. Sample.Title: Capture-C-commd3_mutant_prometa-rep1

Channel.Treatment-Protocol: Cells were synchronized to pro-metaphase with 200ng/ml nocodazole for 7-8.5h. For post-mitotic enrichment, cells were released from nocodazole for 0, 25min, 60min, 120min and 240min for pro-meta, ana/telo, early-G1, mid-G1 and late-G1 respectively.
Channel.Growth-Protocol: G1E-ER4 is a subline of the GATA1-null proerythroblast cell line G1E (Weiss et al, Mol. Cell. Biol. 17, 1642–51 (1997).). Cells were cultered in IMDM medium, supplemented with 15% FBS, penicillin/streptomycin, kit ligand, monothioglycerol and Epoetin alpha at 37 degrees with 5% CO2 (as described in Weiss et al., 1997).
Channel.Extract-Protocol: Capture-C experiments were performed as previously described (Hughes et al. Nat. Genet. 2014, Huang et al. Genes Dev. 2017). 3C library preparation was similar to that of in-situ HiC experiments without biotin nucleotide labeling. Briefly, cells were fixed with 1% PFA and sorted at designated cell cycle stages. DNA was directly ligated after DpnII digestion, extracted with pheno-chloroform and fragmented. Ligated DNA was purified, fragmented and converted into 3C library using NEBNext DNA Library Prep Master Mix Set for Illumina (NEB E6040, M0543L, E7335S). After indexation with NEBNext multiplex oligos, DNA was cleaned up with AMPure XP beads and further amplified with Illumina P5 and P7 primers for 6 cycles. To perform oligo pull-down, 3C libraries were completely dried on a thermal cycler at 60℃. Dried DNA was re-constituted with 13ul hybridization mixture (2.5ul nuclease free water, 7.5ul 2xhybridization buffer, 3ul hybridization component A, Nimblegen SeqCap Hybridization and Wash kit) for 10min at room temperature. DNA was denatured at 95℃ for 10min and then added with 2ul 3pmole biotin-labeled capture probe pool to make a 15ul hybridization system. Hybridization was immediately carried out at 47℃ for more than 24h on a thermal cycler. Hybridized DNA was pulled down with Dynabeads MyOne Streptavidin C1 beads (40ul per capture) and washed with Nimblegen SeqCap Hybridization and Wash kit according to manufacture instruction. Captured DNA was then eluted into 25ul 0.125N NaOH, followed by HCl neutralization. Captured DNA was then purified with AMPure XP beads. Purified DNA was then amplified with Illumina P5 and P7 primer for 12 cycles and PCR products were cleaned up with AMPure XP beads. PCR products were dried on a thermal cycler at 60℃ to initiate a similar second round of capture for further enrichment of targeted DNA. DNA was eluted into 25ul nuclease-free water and subject to sequencing with Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using Illumina sequencing reagents according to manufacturer’s instructions.

Sample.Data-Processing: Library strategy: Capture-C Raw read quality were assessed with Trim Galore (a wrapper for FastQC and Cutadapt). Trimmed reads were merged or interleaved using FLASH 1.2.8. Concatenated reads were then in silico digested with DpnII, aligned to mm9 and analyzed using CCAnalyzer3. Interactions were normalized to total interactions to generate bedgraph files for each bait/condition. Bedgraph files from each individual replicate was merged. Genome_build: mm9

Concatenated reads were then in silico digested with DpnII, aligned to mm9 and analyzed using CCAnalyzer3. Interactions were normalized to total interactions to generate bedgraph files for each bait/condition. Bedgraph files from each individual replicate was merged. Genome_build: mm9

Sample.Title: Capture-C-commd3_mutant_mid-G1-rep2

Channel.Treatment-Protocol: Cells were synchronized to pro-metaphase with 200ng/ml nocodazole for 7-8.5h. For post-mitotic enrichment, cells were released from nocodazole for 0, 25min, 60min, 120min and 240min for pro-meta, ana/telo, early-G1, mid-G1 and late-G1 respectively.
Channel.Growth-Protocol: G1E-ER4 is a subline of the GATA1-null proerythroblast cell line G1E (Weiss et al, Mol. Cell. Biol. 17, 1642–51 (1997).). Cells were cultered in IMDM medium, supplemented with 15% FBS, penicillin/streptomycin, kit ligand, monothioglycerol and Epoetin alpha at 37 degrees with 5% CO2 (as described in Weiss et al., 1997).
Channel.Extract-Protocol: Capture-C experiments were performed as previously described (Hughes et al. Nat. Genet. 2014, Huang et al. Genes Dev. 2017). 3C library preparation was similar to that of in-situ HiC experiments without biotin nucleotide labeling. Briefly, cells were fixed with 1% PFA and sorted at designated cell cycle stages. DNA was directly ligated after DpnII digestion, extracted with pheno-chloroform and fragmented. Ligated DNA was purified, fragmented and converted into 3C library using NEBNext DNA Library Prep Master Mix Set for Illumina (NEB E6040, M0543L, E7335S). After indexation with NEBNext multiplex oligos, DNA was cleaned up with AMPure XP beads and further amplified with Illumina P5 and P7 primers for 6 cycles. To perform oligo pull-down, 3C libraries were completely dried on a thermal cycler at 60℃. Dried DNA was re-constituted with 13ul hybridization mixture (2.5ul nuclease free water, 7.5ul 2xhybridization buffer, 3ul hybridization component A, Nimblegen SeqCap Hybridization and Wash kit) for 10min at room temperature. DNA was denatured at 95℃ for 10min and then added with 2ul 3pmole biotin-labeled capture probe pool to make a 15ul hybridization system. Hybridization was immediately carried out at 47℃ for more than 24h on a thermal cycler. Hybridized DNA was pulled down with Dynabeads MyOne Streptavidin C1 beads (40ul per capture) and washed with Nimblegen SeqCap Hybridization and Wash kit according to manufacture instruction. Captured DNA was then eluted into 25ul 0.125N NaOH, followed by HCl neutralization. Captured DNA was then purified with AMPure XP beads. Purified DNA was then amplified with Illumina P5 and P7 primer for 12 cycles and PCR products were cleaned up with AMPure XP beads. PCR products were dried on a thermal cycler at 60℃ to initiate a similar second round of capture for further enrichment of targeted DNA. DNA was eluted into 25ul nuclease-free water and subject to sequencing with Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using Illumina sequencing reagents according to manufacturer’s instructions.

Sample.Data-Processing: Library strategy: Capture-C Raw read quality were assessed with Trim Galore (a wrapper for FastQC and Cutadapt). Trimmed reads were merged or interleaved using FLASH 1.2.8. Concatenated reads were then in silico digested with DpnII, aligned to mm9 and analyzed using CCAnalyzer3. Interactions were normalized to total interactions to generate bedgraph files for each bait/condition. Bedgraph files from each individual replicate was merged. Genome_build: mm9

Sample.Title: ChIP-seq-PolII-prometa-rep1

Channel.Treatment-Protocol: Cells were synchronized to pro-metaphase with 200ng/ml nocodazole for 7-8.5h. For post-mitotic enrichment, cells were released from nocodazole for 0, 25min, 60min, 120min and 240min for pro-meta, ana/telo, early-G1, mid-G1 and late-G1 respectively.
Channel.Growth-Protocol: G1E-ER4 is a subline of the GATA1-null proerythroblast cell line G1E (Weiss et al, Mol. Cell. Biol. 17, 1642–51 (1997).). Cells were cultered in IMDM medium, supplemented with 15% FBS, penicillin/streptomycin, kit ligand, monothioglycerol and Epoetin alpha at 37 degrees with 5% CO2 (as described in Weiss et al., 1997).
Channel.Extract-Protocol: For each IP, 7-10million crosslinked cells were re-suspended in 1ml Cell Lysis Buffer (10mM Tris pH 8, 10mM NaCl, 0.2% NP-40/Igepal) containing fresh protease inhibitors (Sigma P8340) and 1mM phenylmethylsulfonyl fluoride (PMSF) for 20min on ice. Nuclei was pelleted at 4℃ and re-suspended in 1ml Nuclear Lysis Buffer (50mM Tris pH 8, 10mM EDTA, 1% SDS) containing fresh protease inhibitors and PMSF and incubated for 20min on ice. Samples were added with 0.6ml IP Dilution Buffer (20mM Tris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 150mM NaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.01% SDS) containing fresh protease inhibitor and PMSF and then subject to sonication for 45min (Epishear, Active Motif, 100% amplitude, 30s ON and 30s OFF, ~45min). After sonication, samples were spun at 21130g at 4℃ for 10min to pellet cell debris. Supernatant was transferred to a new tube and supplemented with 3.4ml IP Dilution Buffer containing fresh protease inhibitor, PMSF, protein A/G agarose beads (prepared by mixing protein A (Thermo Fisher Scientific 15918014) and protein G (Thermo Fisher Scientific 15920010) agarose beads at 1:1 ratio) and 50mg isotope matched IgG. Chromatin preclear was carried out for over 2h at 4℃. 200ml (4%) of precleared chromatin was taken out from subsequent IP steps and set as input. Pre-cleared chromatin was then added to protein A/C beads bound with antibodies and incubated overnight with rotation at 4℃. After IP, beads were washed once with IP Wash Buffer I (20mM Tris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 50mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.1% SDS), twice with High Salt Buffer (20mMTris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 500mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.01% SDS), once with IP Wash Buffer II (10mMTris pH 8, 1mM EDTA, 0.25 M LiCl, 1% NP-40/Igepal, 1% sodium deoxycholate) and twice with PE Buffer (10mM Tris pH 8, 1mM EDTA pH 8). All washing and pelleting steps were performed on ice or at 4℃. Beads were then moved to room temperature and eluted twice with 100ml freshly prepared Elution Buffer (100mM NaHCO3, 1%SDS) to reach a final concentration of 200ml. Eluted samples were supplemented with 12ml 5M NaCl, 2ml DNase-free RNaseA (10mg/ml, 10109169001 BMB) and incubated at 65℃ for overnight. Samples were then added with 3ml protease K (20mg/ml, 3115879 BMB) and further de-crosslinked at 65℃ for 2h. De-crosslinked DNA was added with 10ml 3M sodium acetate pH 5.2 and purified with QIAquick PCR Purification kit (QIAGEN 28106) according to manufacture instruction. Library construction was performed using Illumina’s TruSeq ChIP sample preparation kit (Illumina, catalog no. IP-202-1012) according to manufacturer’s specifications with the addition of size selection (left side at 0.9x, right side at 0.6x) using SPRIselect beads (Beckman Coulter, catalog no. B23318) prior to PCR amplification. Library size was determined (average 351 bp, range 333-372 bp) using the Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100, followed by quantitation using real-time PCR using the KAPA Library Quant Kit for Illumina (KAPA Biosystems catalog no. KK4835). Libraries were then pooled and sequenced on the Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using Illumina sequencing reagents according to manufacturer’s instructions

Sample.Data-Processing: Sequencing reads were mapped to the reference mouse genome mm9 using bowtie (0.12.7, "-m 2 --tryhard"). Reads were filtered to remove non-uniquely mapped reads and PCR duplicates using SAMtools (v1.7, "samtools rmdup -s") and converted to bed format using BEDtools (v2.27.1, "bedtools bamtobed"). Peaks were identified using the MACS2 (v2.1.0, "macs2 callpeak") with punctate calling for both CTCF and Rad21 (p-values 1e-8 and 1e-4 respectively), using each IP's cell cycle stage matched input as the control. Genome_build: mm9

All washing and pelleting steps were performed on ice or at 4℃. Beads were then moved to room temperature and eluted twice with 100ml freshly prepared Elution Buffer (100mM NaHCO3, 1%SDS) to reach a final concentration of 200ml. Eluted samples were supplemented with 12ml 5M NaCl, 2ml DNase-free RNaseA (10mg/ml, 10109169001 BMB) and incubated at 65℃ for overnight. Samples were then added with 3ml protease K (20mg/ml, 3115879 BMB) and further de-crosslinked at 65℃ for 2h. De-crosslinked DNA was added with 10ml 3M sodium acetate pH 5.2 and purified with QIAquick PCR Purification kit (QIAGEN 28106) according to manufacture instruction. Library construction was performed using Illumina’s TruSeq ChIP sample preparation kit (Illumina, catalog no. IP-202-1012) according to manufacturer’s specifications with the addition of size selection (left side at 0.9x, right side at 0.6x) using SPRIselect beads (Beckman Coulter, catalog no. B23318) prior to PCR amplification. Library size was determined (average 351 bp, range 333-372 bp) using the Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100, followed by quantitation using real-time PCR using the KAPA Library Quant Kit for Illumina (KAPA Biosystems catalog no. KK4835). Libraries were then pooled and sequenced on the Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using Illumina sequencing reagents according to manufacturer’s instructions

Sample.Data-Processing: Sequencing reads were mapped to the reference mouse genome mm9 using bowtie (0.12.7, "-m 2 --tryhard"). Reads were filtered to remove non-uniquely mapped reads and PCR duplicates using SAMtools (v1.7, "samtools rmdup -s") and converted to bed format using BEDtools (v2.27.1, "bedtools bamtobed"). Peaks were identified using the MACS2 (v2.1.0, "macs2 callpeak") with punctate calling for both CTCF and Rad21 (p-values 1e-8 and 1e-4 respectively), using each IP's cell cycle stage matched input as the control. Genome_build: mm9

Library size was determined (average 351 bp, range 333-372 bp) using the Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100, followed by quantitation using real-time PCR using the KAPA Library Quant Kit for Illumina (KAPA Biosystems catalog no. KK4835). Libraries were then pooled and sequenced on the Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using Illumina sequencing reagents according to manufacturer’s instructions

Sample.Data-Processing: Sequencing reads were mapped to the reference mouse genome mm9 using bowtie (0.12.7, "-m 2 --tryhard"). Reads were filtered to remove non-uniquely mapped reads and PCR duplicates using SAMtools (v1.7, "samtools rmdup -s") and converted to bed format using BEDtools (v2.27.1, "bedtools bamtobed"). Peaks were identified using the MACS2 (v2.1.0, "macs2 callpeak") with punctate calling for both CTCF and Rad21 (p-values 1e-8 and 1e-4 respectively), using each IP's cell cycle stage matched input as the control. Genome_build: mm9

Sample.Title: ChIP-seq-PolII-late-g1-rep2

Channel.Treatment-Protocol: Cells were synchronized to pro-metaphase with 200ng/ml nocodazole for 7-8.5h. For post-mitotic enrichment, cells were released from nocodazole for 0, 25min, 60min, 120min and 240min for pro-meta, ana/telo, early-G1, mid-G1 and late-G1 respectively.
Channel.Growth-Protocol: G1E-ER4 is a subline of the GATA1-null proerythroblast cell line G1E (Weiss et al, Mol. Cell. Biol. 17, 1642–51 (1997).). Cells were cultered in IMDM medium, supplemented with 15% FBS, penicillin/streptomycin, kit ligand, monothioglycerol and Epoetin alpha at 37 degrees with 5% CO2 (as described in Weiss et al., 1997).
Channel.Extract-Protocol: For each IP, 7-10million crosslinked cells were re-suspended in 1ml Cell Lysis Buffer (10mM Tris pH 8, 10mM NaCl, 0.2% NP-40/Igepal) containing fresh protease inhibitors (Sigma P8340) and 1mM phenylmethylsulfonyl fluoride (PMSF) for 20min on ice. Nuclei was pelleted at 4℃ and re-suspended in 1ml Nuclear Lysis Buffer (50mM Tris pH 8, 10mM EDTA, 1% SDS) containing fresh protease inhibitors and PMSF and incubated for 20min on ice. Samples were added with 0.6ml IP Dilution Buffer (20mM Tris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 150mM NaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.01% SDS) containing fresh protease inhibitor and PMSF and then subject to sonication for 45min (Epishear, Active Motif, 100% amplitude, 30s ON and 30s OFF, ~45min). After sonication, samples were spun at 21130g at 4℃ for 10min to pellet cell debris. Supernatant was transferred to a new tube and supplemented with 3.4ml IP Dilution Buffer containing fresh protease inhibitor, PMSF, protein A/G agarose beads (prepared by mixing protein A (Thermo Fisher Scientific 15918014) and protein G (Thermo Fisher Scientific 15920010) agarose beads at 1:1 ratio) and 50mg isotope matched IgG. Chromatin preclear was carried out for over 2h at 4℃. 200ml (4%) of precleared chromatin was taken out from subsequent IP steps and set as input. Pre-cleared chromatin was then added to protein A/C beads bound with antibodies and incubated overnight with rotation at 4℃. After IP, beads were washed once with IP Wash Buffer I (20mM Tris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 50mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.1% SDS), twice with High Salt Buffer (20mMTris pH 8, 2mM EDTA, 500mMNaCl, 1% Triton X-100, 0.01% SDS), once with IP Wash Buffer II (10mMTris pH 8, 1mM EDTA, 0.25 M LiCl, 1% NP-40/Igepal, 1% sodium deoxycholate) and twice with PE Buffer (10mM Tris pH 8, 1mM EDTA pH 8). All washing and pelleting steps were performed on ice or at 4℃. Beads were then moved to room temperature and eluted twice with 100ml freshly prepared Elution Buffer (100mM NaHCO3, 1%SDS) to reach a final concentration of 200ml. Eluted samples were supplemented with 12ml 5M NaCl, 2ml DNase-free RNaseA (10mg/ml, 10109169001 BMB) and incubated at 65℃ for overnight. Samples were then added with 3ml protease K (20mg/ml, 3115879 BMB) and further de-crosslinked at 65℃ for 2h. De-crosslinked DNA was added with 10ml 3M sodium acetate pH 5.2 and purified with QIAquick PCR Purification kit (QIAGEN 28106) according to manufacture instruction. Library construction was performed using Illumina’s TruSeq ChIP sample preparation kit (Illumina, catalog no. IP-202-1012) according to manufacturer’s specifications with the addition of size selection (left side at 0.9x, right side at 0.6x) using SPRIselect beads (Beckman Coulter, catalog no. B23318) prior to PCR amplification. Library size was determined (average 351 bp, range 333-372 bp) using the Agilent Bioanalyzer 2100, followed by quantitation using real-time PCR using the KAPA Library Quant Kit for Illumina (KAPA Biosystems catalog no. KK4835). Libraries were then pooled and sequenced on the Illumina NextSeq 500 platform using Illumina sequencing reagents according to manufacturer’s instructions

Sample.Data-Processing: Sequencing reads were mapped to the reference mouse genome mm9 using bowtie (0.12.7, "-m 2 --tryhard"). Reads were filtered to remove non-uniquely mapped reads and PCR duplicates using SAMtools (v1.7, "samtools rmdup -s") and converted to bed format using BEDtools (v2.27.1, "bedtools bamtobed"). Peaks were identified using the MACS2 (v2.1.0, "macs2 callpeak") with punctate calling for both CTCF and Rad21 (p-values 1e-8 and 1e-4 respectively), using each IP's cell cycle stage matched input as the control. Genome_build: mm9

Series.Title: Re-configuration of Chromatin Structure During the Mitosis-G1 Phase Progression
Series.Summary: Chromatin architecture is temporarily disrupted during mitosis. Yet, how chromatin architecture is reconfigured during M-G1 phase transition is unknown. To examine this, we performed Hi-C experiments on cell populations at defined cell cycle stages between mitosis and G1 phase.
Series.Overall-Design: Uninduced G1E-ER4 (-GATA1) cells were synchronized to pro-metaphase with nocodazole treatment and was released from nocodazole for a variety of durations. Cells at different cell cycle stages were purified with FACS. Hi-C experiments were performed on purified populations to examine chromatin structure at designated cell cycle stage.
Series.Type: Genome binding/occupancy profiling by high throughput sequencing
